title: Embedded methods
key_concepts: Embedded methods perform feature selection naturally during the model training process itself.
They combine the speed of filter methods with the model-specific accuracy of wrapper methods.

LASSO (L1 regularization) adds a penalty equal to the absolute magnitude of coefficients. This unique geometric property shrinks less important feature coefficients to exactly zero, effectively selecting features.
Tree-based models (like Random Forest) provide built-in feature importance by tracking how much each feature decreases node impurity across all trees.

Boruta is a robust extension of tree-based importance. It creates 'shadow features' (random permutations of original features) to set a statistical baseline, selecting only features that perform significantly better than random noise.

definitions.Embedded methods: Metode seleksi fitur yang terintegrasi langsung di dalam proses pelatihan model.

examples: Contrast L1 (LASSO) with L2 (Ridge) conceptually: explain why L1 zeros out coefficients while L2 only shrinks them close to zero.
relations: Bridges the gap between feature selection and model training.
Ties into Chapter 9's discussion of feature importance (model-based importance).
exclude: Mathematical derivation of L1 vs L2 geometry (keep it intuitive).
